Serving Southern California since 2014

New clothes.
Real dignity.
God's love in action.

Nothing wasted. Everything given.

NC4GK connects Southern California brands with families in genuine need, turning surplus inventory into dignity one piece of clothing at a time.

$10M+Clothing Distributed
403KPieces of Clothing
193+Gifts per $100
100%No Resale. Ever.

All volunteer-run. Less than $25,000 per year in operating costs. Every dollar goes directly to getting clothes where they belong.

Our Mission

Where surplus finds its purpose

Every season, apparel brands are left with inventory that cannot move. Surplus runs, cosmetic irregulars, samples that never shipped. Putting it into the marketplace means competing with their own customers. Discarding it means waste. NC4GK offers a third way.

Across Southern California, millions of families live in genuine need. NC4GK exists at the intersection of these two realities, gathering what brands cannot use and delivering it, through verified Christian partners, to the families who need it most.

All product is given. No product is ever sold.The NC4GK Promise

See the impact

Hundreds of families.
One block. One day.

Aerial footage coming

The Need

Southern California is one of the wealthiest places on earth. The need is still extraordinary.

California leads the nation in poverty when accounting for the true cost of living. In Los Angeles County, nearly one in three young children lives below the poverty line. NC4GK exists to close the gap between surplus and need.

Sources: California Budget and Policy Center (2024); U.S. Census Bureau, Supplemental Poverty Measure; Public Policy Institute of California

18.6%

California child poverty rate

The highest in the nation when accounting for cost of living. (U.S. Census, 2024 SPM)

~30%

Young children in poverty in LA County

Nearly 1 in 3 children under 5 in Los Angeles County live below the poverty line. (PPIC)

7M+

Californians living in poverty in 2024

More than the combined populations of LA, San Diego, San Jose, and San Francisco. (U.S. Census, 2024 SPM)

Our Story

Where surplus finds its purpose

The Problem We Solve

Every season, brands are left with inventory they cannot move.

The apparel industry is built on margin, and surplus product threatens that margin the moment it hits the wrong channel. Overruns, cosmetic irregulars, off-spec runs, samples, end-of-season stock. Brands cannot put it into the marketplace without competing with their own customers. They cannot just throw it away. So it sits.

Southern California is home to a remarkable concentration of apparel and action sports companies. And it is also home to some of the deepest pockets of poverty in the country. NC4GK was founded on one simple insight: these two realities could solve each other.

The Need We Meet

Families across Southern California are waiting for what brands cannot sell.

Disaster victims. Families in shelters. Children in underserved communities across Santa Ana, Anaheim, Lancaster, Hollywood, Van Nuys, Riverside, South-Central LA. The need is consistent, deep, and close to home.

NC4GK coordinates with Salvation Army shelters, City Serve, and other verified Christian organizations to ensure every item gets to someone in genuine need. Not a discount bin, not a resale channel. Every item is a gift.

"Since 2014 the Salvation Army has distributed new clothes to people in need with NC4GK. We greatly value and appreciate our partnership, serving together to make a difference in the lives of others."
Kevin White, Past Communications Director, Salvation Army Orange County
How It Works

Simple process.
Real impact.

Over a decade refining a system that is effortless for brand partners and ensures every item reaches the right hands.

01

Brand donates inventory

Surplus, cosmetic irregulars, or off-spec runs collected from brand partners. We coordinate logistics so it is effortless on your end.

02

We intake and store

Every item is received, sorted, and held until matched to the right distribution event. This is where the real operational work happens.

Highest-cost step. Financial gifts help most here.
03

Distribution is coordinated

We work with verified Christian partners to plan distribution, matching inventory to communities and families in genuine need, careful not to create dependency.

04

Clothes reach families

Through trusted organizations, new clothing is placed directly in the hands of children and families, with a 100% no-resale guarantee at every step.*

* See the NC4GK Guarantee below.
Distribution Partners

Where the clothes go

Every partner holds a Christian mission and a verified commitment to direct distribution, not resale. We distribute in ways that empower rather than create dependency.

The Salvation Army

Shelters and community centers across Santa Ana, Anaheim, City of Bell, Lancaster, Hollywood, Van Nuys, Cathedral City, Riverside, and South-Central LA. Serving veterans, the homeless, and families in crisis.

City Serve

Disaster relief and community outreach. NC4GK has partnered with City Serve to reach Altadena fire victims, Lancaster Riverbed encampments, and other emergency situations across Southern California.

Know a Christian organization or community we should serve?

We are always expanding our partner network. Whether you represent a faith-based organization looking to partner, or know a shelter, school, or neighborhood in need, reach out.

Suggest a Community

The NC4GK Guarantee*: Every distribution partner is personally vetted and holds a Christian mission. All clothing goes directly to families in need, never to a resale channel. This is our promise to every brand that trusts us with their inventory.

* While NC4GK personally vets every distribution partner and requires a commitment to direct, non-commercial distribution, we acknowledge that we cannot control the actions of individual recipients once clothing has been distributed.

By the Numbers

Over a decade of doing this right

Every number represents a real family, a real child, a real moment of dignity.

Less than $25,000 per year in operating costs. NC4GK is entirely volunteer-run. Generous product donors and volunteers allow a tremendous return on every dollar donated.

$10M+Clothing distributed
403KPieces of clothing
193+Gifts per $100
0%Resale. Ever.
Brand Partners

We help brands do good with what they cannot sell

Cosmetic irregulars, misprints, off-spec runs, surplus. We give it a verified home where it becomes a gift, not a liability.

Cosmetic Irregulars

Misprints, stitching variances, colorway errors. Items that cannot go through retail without impacting brand perception.

Surplus Inventory

Overruns, end-of-season stock, excess production that would otherwise sit in a warehouse or go to liquidation.

Off-Spec Goods

Anything new and wearable that does not belong in your normal channels. We make sure it reaches someone who truly needs it.

Current Partners

25+ brands.
One mission.

These companies have chosen to turn surplus inventory into something meaningful. We are proud to call them partners.

Ready to put your inventory to work?

Tell us what you have and we will walk you through the rest. Simple process, verified distribution, zero resale, ever.

Upcoming Events

Mark your calendar

NC4GK holds multiple distribution events throughout the year. Event frequency and scale adjust to the volume of inventory we receive. The more brands donate, the more communities we can serve.

Field Notes

Updates, stories and dispatches from the work

Distribution recaps, community moments, event stories, and the people behind the mission.

Distribution

Back-to-School 2023, South Central Los Angeles

Hundreds of families. One afternoon. One block.

At the Salvation Army Youth and Community Center on Central Avenue, families lined the block in the summer heat. Umbrellas out, kids in tow, waiting for new clothing before the school year. NC4GK coordinated the inventory, the partners handled the distribution, and every item found a home.

Full story coming. Tim's backstory to be added here.

Community

Lancaster, California

Lancaster Riverbed. Meeting people where they are.

Not every distribution happens inside a community center. NC4GK and its partners take the work to the places that need it most. Encampments, riverbeds, street corners. This is what it looks like when faith becomes action.

Full story coming. Tim's backstory to be added here.

Disaster Relief

Altadena Fire Response, 2025

The LA fires created an urgent need. Brands stepped up.

When the Altadena fires displaced thousands of families, NC4GK worked with City Serve to connect brands holding surplus inventory with relief efforts on the ground. The clothing pipeline that normally serves scheduled distributions became an emergency response system.

Full story coming. Tim's backstory to be added here.

Field Report

South Central Los Angeles

The murals, the streets, and the communities NC4GK calls home.

The neighborhoods where NC4GK works are places of deep culture, deep faith, and deep need. The murals on the walls tell the story of communities that have survived and are still here. So is NC4GK.

Full story coming. Tim's backstory to be added here.

Please note: NC4GK is a B2B inventory partner. We work exclusively with brands and manufacturers donating new clothing in pallet or bulk quantities. For individual or used items, please connect with your local Salvation Army or thrift store. We appreciate your generosity either way.
